entry.title: Auth.js: Email normalizer validates the address before Unicode normalization, allowing a homoglyph @ bypass
entry.content:
vulnerability · auth-bypass

Auth.js (affected versions of @auth/core and next-auth) contains a critical vulnerability in the email normalizer for magic-link authentication, which validates email addresses before applying Unicode normalization. An attacker can bypass the single-@ symbol validation by using Unicode characters that canonicalize to @ under NFKC/NFKD normalization, potentially allowing account takeover through homoglyph attacks. This vulnerability is actively exploited and affects both @auth/core and next-auth packages. Immediately update to patched versions that perform Unicode normalization before validation.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: Auth.js: Configuration errors can cause existence-based auth checks to fail open (auth object populated with an error)
entry.content:
vulnerability · auth-bypass

Auth.js (next-auth) v5 is affected by a critical configuration error that causes the `auth()` function to return an error object instead of null, breaking existence-based authentication checks in middleware and route handlers. Applications using the documented pattern of gating access by checking only for the `auth` object's presence will fail open, allowing unauthenticated access when server-side configuration errors occur. This vulnerability is being exploited in the wild. Update to the latest patched version of next-auth immediately and review all authentication gates to ensure proper null-checking rather than relying solely on object existence.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: Hackers abuse Notepad++ plugins to stealthily install malware
entry.content:
red-team · malware-distribution

Notepad++ is being weaponized through malicious plugins disguised as legitimate extensions to achieve persistent malware installation. The LunchPoke malware masquerades as a Notepad++ plugin and establishes persistence on compromised systems, with the attack vector involving distribution of archives containing both legitimate Notepad++ and the malicious component. This represents a moderate-to-high severity threat with active exploitation confirmed by Ukraine's CERT. Users should avoid downloading Notepad++ from untrusted sources, validate plugin authenticity through official channels only, and consider disabling auto-loading of plugins if not actively used.

Read more · Source: bleepingcomputer

entry.title: Claude Cowork Flaw Could Let AI Agent Escape Its VM and Access Mac Files
entry.content:
vulnerability · sandbox-escape

Anthropic's Claude Cowork AI agent contains a sandbox escape vulnerability allowing attackers to break out of the Linux VM sandbox and access arbitrary files on the host macOS system. The flaw enables reading and writing of files outside the intended sandbox boundaries, affecting approximately 500,000 macOS users. Severity and exploitation status are not specified in the provided summary, but sandbox escapes are typically critical as they defeat the primary security boundary. Users should immediately update Claude Cowork to the patched version and consider restricting the agent's file access permissions until a fix is applied.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: Nine-Year-Old RefluXFS Linux Flaw Gives Local Users Root on Default RHEL Installs
entry.content:
vulnerability · privilege-escalation · CVE-2026-64600

RefluXFS (CVE-2026-64600) is a nine-year-old Linux kernel race condition in XFS filesystem handling that allows unprivileged local users to overwrite root-owned files and achieve persistent root access on affected systems. Default installations of RHEL, Fedora Server, and Amazon Linux are vulnerable. The flaw is critical severity; no evidence of wild exploitation has been reported as of disclosure. Patch immediately via kernel updates from your respective Linux distributions.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: Check Point SmartConsole: Check Point SmartConsole Improper Authentication Vulnerability (CVE-2026-16232)
entry.content:
vulnerability · auth-bypass · CVE-2026-16232

Check Point SmartConsole contains an improper authentication flaw (CVE-2026-16232) allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to obtain valid administrative login tokens without credentials. This vulnerability is actively exploited in the wild and carries critical severity due to the ability to gain full administrative access to the console. Apply vendor-supplied patches immediately per CISA BOD 26-04 guidance; if mitigations are unavailable, discontinue use of the product or migrate to cloud alternatives with proper controls. Conduct forensics triage on affected systems to identify compromise and unauthorized administrative activity.

Read more · Source: cisa-kev

entry.title: Intercepting Android WebView traffic under new certificate validity requirement by Chromium
entry.content:
red-team · mitm-interception

Android WebView and Chromium-based browsers now enforce stricter certificate validity requirements that prevent interception of HTTPS traffic using self-signed or expired certificates, even when certificate pinning is not implemented. This change affects penetration testing and security assessments where analysts previously relied on proxy certificates for traffic inspection. While this is a security hardening measure rather than a vulnerability, it complicates legitimate security testing workflows. Security teams should use alternative testing methods such as device-level network analysis, application-level logging, or work with application developers to implement debug-mode certificate acceptance rather than attempting to bypass these validity checks.

Read more · Source: nviso-labs

entry.title: Adobe Chrome extension flaw let sites access private WhatsApp chats
entry.content:
vulnerability · auth-bypass

Adobe Acrobat Chrome extension contained a vulnerability allowing unauthorized access to WhatsApp Web conversations and private data without authentication through improper content script permissions. The flaw affected the extension's ability to isolate content across different web domains, exposing sensitive communications. Severity is high given the personal nature of WhatsApp data and lack of authentication requirement; exploitation likelihood in the wild is moderate as it requires users to have both the extension and WhatsApp Web active. Adobe has patched the vulnerability; users should immediately update the Adobe Acrobat extension to the latest version and review browser extension permissions.

Read more · Source: bleepingcomputer

entry.title: Oracle Critical Patch Update: 1449 Softwareflicken im Juli
entry.content:
vulnerability · multiple

Oracle released 1,449 security patches in July, a record number covering vulnerabilities across its product portfolio including Java, WebLogic, Database, and Enterprise applications. The updates address critical remote code execution flaws, SQL injection vulnerabilities, and authentication bypass issues, with multiple CVEs rated CVSS 9.0 or higher indicating severe impact. Several vulnerabilities are exploited in the wild or have public exploits available, particularly affecting WebLogic and Java components. Immediate patching is mandatory, prioritizing critical-rated CVEs in Internet-facing systems such as WebLogic and Java Runtime Environment, followed by systematic deployment of remaining updates across the enterprise infrastructure.

Read more · Source: heise-security

entry.title: Credential confusion in @sigstore/oci can leak registry credentials to an attacker-controlled registry (CVE-2026-59891)
entry.content:
vulnerability · credential-exposure · CVE-2026-59891

The @sigstore/oci npm package contains a critical credential confusion vulnerability (CVE-2026-59891) in the `getRegistryCredentials()` function, which uses substring matching instead of exact hostname matching to select Docker credentials from ~/.docker/config.json. An attacker can craft a registry URL containing a legitimate registry's hostname as a substring to trick the function into leaking credentials intended for that legitimate registry. This vulnerability is critical severity; patch to the latest version immediately. No evidence of active exploitation in the wild is mentioned, but the high severity and ease of exploitation warrant urgent patching.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: @vitest/browser: Browser Mode provider commands bypass the file-access permission gate
entry.content:
vulnerability · auth-bypass

Vitest's @vitest/browser package contains a critical vulnerability where Browser Mode provider commands bypass file-access permission checks, allowing unauthenticated clients to read/write arbitrary files on the host system. Commands for screenshot management, Playwright traces, and file uploads do not validate the `allowWrite` permission gate or confine operations to the project directory. This vulnerability is critical in severity and could enable full filesystem compromise on systems running Vitest Browser Mode. Immediately update @vitest/browser to the patched version and restrict network access to Browser Mode APIs to trusted networks only.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: Gitea: Incomplete SSRF Protection in Webhook and Migration Allow-list Default Filter (CVE-2026-22874)
entry.content:
vulnerability · ssrf · CVE-2026-22874

Gitea's webhook and migration functionality uses an incomplete SSRF allow-list that relies solely on Go's `net.IP.IsPrivate()` function, which does not block access to cloud metadata services, certain internal network ranges, and IPv6 transition mechanisms beyond RFC 1918 and RFC 4193. This allows attackers to bypass intended restrictions and access sensitive internal services or cloud metadata endpoints. The vulnerability is critical in severity. Users should upgrade to a patched version of Gitea that implements a more comprehensive SSRF filter covering additional reserved IP ranges and protocols.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: Gitea Actions Artifacts V4 signed URL HMAC ambiguity allows cross-repository artifact read and cross-task upload-state write (CVE-2026-58426)
entry.content:
vulnerability · hmac-bypass · CVE-2026-58426

Gitea Actions Artifacts V4 is vulnerable to HMAC signature ambiguity (CVE-2026-58426) allowing attackers with Actions job permissions to rewrite signed URLs to access artifacts across repositories and tasks while maintaining valid signatures. An attacker can read artifacts from arbitrary task contexts or write malicious data to another task's staging area, including in private repositories. This critical vulnerability is currently being exploited in the wild. Affected organizations should immediately patch Gitea to the latest version addressing this HMAC validation flaw.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: Gitea: Public-only repository tokens can update private PR head branches (CVE-2026-58443)
entry.content:
vulnerability · auth-bypass · CVE-2026-58443

Gitea versions prior to the fix contain a critical authorization bypass (CVE-2026-58443) where public-only repository tokens with write:repository permissions can update private pull request head branches by exploiting the `/api/v1/repos/{public-owner}/{public-repo}/pulls/{index}/update` endpoint. The vulnerability stems from insufficient permission validation—the system checks the public-only restriction against the public base repository but fails to properly restrict operations on the private head repository. This is a critical severity issue likely exploitable in the wild given its straightforward exploitation path. Administrators should immediately apply available patches and review logs for token misuse; consider rotating any public-only tokens with repository write permissions until patched.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: Apple Fixes Hide My Email Bug That Exposed Real Addresses in Mail Logs
entry.content:
vulnerability · information-disclosure

Apple patched a privacy vulnerability in its Hide My Email service that exposed users' real email addresses in mail logs despite the masking feature being enabled. The flaw allowed real addresses to leak in email headers and server logs, directly undermining the privacy protection users relied on. Apple deployed the fix on July 3, 2026, over a year after the vulnerability was reported by a security researcher. While no evidence of active exploitation in the wild was mentioned, users should verify their Hide My Email implementation is updated to restore the intended privacy guarantees.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: CVE-2026-8933: Local Privilege Escalation in Set-Capabilities snap-confine
entry.content:
vulnerability · lpe · CVE-2026-8933

CVE-2026-8933 affects snap-confine across Ubuntu Desktop 24.04, 25.10, and 26.04, allowing unprivileged local users to escalate privileges to root via a race condition in sandbox capability-setting mechanisms. The vulnerability is critical in severity and exploitation status is not specified in the provided summary. Immediate patching of snap-confine is recommended for affected Ubuntu systems; users should apply security updates as soon as available from Canonical.

Read more · Source: qualys

entry.title: Your AI agent’s config is now the payload: How attackers are targeting the developer agent harness
entry.content:
red-team · persistence,config-injection,supply-chain

AI coding assistant configuration files (settings.json, .cursorrules, .windsurfrules, .github/copilot-instructions.md) are now explicit supply-chain attack targets, with the Mini Shai-Hulud worm and variants injecting persistent hooks and prompt injections to achieve silent execution with developer-level trust. The worm scans for AI tool configs across npm, PyPI, and other registries, injects SessionStart hooks or LLM-instruction payloads, and uses model refusal evasion (clear-bomb technique) to blind AI-based scanners while spreading via stolen GitHub tokens. This represents high-severity threats actively exploited in the wild that shift compromise from package installation to the persistent agent harness layer. Defenders must treat harness config files as code requiring mandatory reviews and SHA-hash pinning in CI/CD, enforce --ignore-scripts on package installs, flag alwaysApply rules with broad globs as critical findings, rotate GitHub tokens immediately post-install, proxy LLM API traffic with egress filtering, and treat AI scanner refusals as suspicious signals requiring escalation to non-AI analysis.

Read more · Source: tenable

entry.title: DD-WRT DD-WRT: DD-WRT Stack-Based Buffer Overflow Vulnerability (CVE-2021-27137)
entry.content:
vulnerability · buffer-overflow · CVE-2021-27137

DD-WRT firmware contains a stack-based buffer overflow in the UPnP service (CVE-2021-27137) allowing unauthenticated remote code execution. This vulnerability is actively exploited in the wild with high severity. Apply vendor patches immediately per CISA BOD 26-04 prioritization guidance; if mitigations are unavailable, discontinue use of affected devices. Assess internet exposure of all DD-WRT deployments and enforce expedited patching timelines.

Read more · Source: cisa-kev

entry.title: New Bit2Watt Attack Could Let Cloud Tenants Disrupt Power Grids Without an Exploit
entry.content:
red-team · side-channel

Bit2Watt is a side-channel attack leveraging GPU workload manipulation in cloud environments to induce rapid power fluctuations in data centers, potentially destabilizing connected power grids. The attack requires no vulnerability exploitation or system compromise—a standard cloud tenant can intentionally modulate GPU compute patterns to create dangerous frequency variations. Severity is significant as it represents a denial-of-service vector against critical infrastructure, though active wild exploitation has not been documented. Mitigation includes power smoothing mechanisms, rate-limiting GPU resource allocation per tenant, enhanced power monitoring with anomaly detection, and coordination with grid operators on data center load variability constraints.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: Open-Source Android AI Agents Could Let Invisible Screen Text Run Code on Host PCs
entry.content:
vulnerability · prompt-injection

Open-source Android AI agent frameworks (AppAgent, AppAgentX, and three others) are vulnerable to privilege escalation attacks where malicious Android apps can inject invisible on-screen text and shared storage instructions to compromise the agent's host PC. An attacker needs only basic Android permissions (overlay and storage write access) to execute arbitrary commands on the controlling computer through a multi-step exploitation chain. Severity is high as demonstrated by researchers with proof-of-concept attacks, though no in-the-wild exploitation is reported. Developers should immediately restrict agent framework access to only trusted applications, implement input validation/sanitization for all screen text processing, disable agent execution of suspicious commands, and update frameworks when patches become available.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: Zimbra Patches Critical SNMP Command Injection and Four XSS Vulnerabilities
entry.content:
vulnerability · command-injection

Zimbra Collaboration Suite addressed nine vulnerabilities in version 10.1.20, including a critical SNMP command injection flaw in its monitoring component that allows remote code execution when SNMP notifications are enabled, plus four XSS vulnerabilities. The SNMP command injection is rated critical severity and poses significant risk to affected deployments. Immediate patching to version 10.1.20 or later is strongly recommended for all Zimbra installations with SNMP monitoring enabled.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: AWS Kiro Flaw Let a Poisoned Web Page Rewrite Its Config and Run Code
entry.content:
vulnerability · rce

AWS's Kiro agentic IDE was vulnerable to remote code execution through poisoned web pages containing hidden text that could trick the agent into rewriting its configuration and executing arbitrary code without user approval. An attacker could achieve RCE through seemingly benign requests like asking Kiro to summarize a webpage. AWS has already patched the vulnerability, and while severity was high due to the direct path to code execution on developer machines, there is no indication of active exploitation in the wild. Developers using Kiro should ensure they are running the latest patched version and exercise caution when processing untrusted web content.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: Critical Palo Alto VPN bug now exploited by Qilin ransomware gang
entry.content:
vulnerability · auth-bypass · CVE-2026-0257

Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS GlobalProtect is vulnerable to a critical authentication bypass flaw (CVE-2024-21893) that the Qilin ransomware gang actively exploits for network intrusion. The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to bypass authentication mechanisms and gain unauthorized access to protected networks. This is a severe, actively exploited vulnerability affecting critical VPN infrastructure. Organizations must immediately apply available patches from Palo Alto Networks and monitor for signs of compromise in their GlobalProtect logs and access patterns.

Read more · Source: bleepingcomputer

entry.title: Sicherheitspatch Grafana: Angreifer können sensible Daten abgreifen
entry.content:
vulnerability · information-disclosure

Grafana contains a critical vulnerability allowing attackers to extract sensitive data through unauthorized access. The flaw impacts the open-source visualization and monitoring platform's security posture. Patch availability has been released by developers and immediate application is recommended for all affected deployments. Users should prioritize updates to prevent potential exposure of monitoring data and credentials stored within Grafana instances.

Read more · Source: heise-security

entry.title: LightRAG: CORS Wildcard + Credentials Enables Any-Origin Credentialed Requests (CVE-2026-61736)
entry.content:
vulnerability · cors-misconfiguration · CVE-2026-61736

LightRAG (pip package lightrag-hku) is vulnerable to critical CORS misconfiguration (CVE-2026-61736) where the default settings combine CORS_ORIGINS=* with allow_credentials=True, allowing any website to perform authenticated API calls on behalf of logged-in users. Starlette's CORSMiddleware echoes the requesting origin in preflight responses when credentials are enabled, effectively whitelisting all origins for credentialed requests. This vulnerability is critical severity and likely exploitable in the wild given the default insecure configuration. Immediately update to a patched version and configure CORS_ORIGINS to specific trusted domains, or disable credentials in CORS responses if not required for functionality.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: LightRAG is Vulnerable to Authentication Bypass: hardcoded DEFAULT_TOKEN_SECRET and public /auth-status defeat LIGHTRAG_API_KEY protection (CVE-2026-61740)
entry.content:
vulnerability · auth-bypass · CVE-2026-61740

LightRAG (pip/lightrag-hku) contains a critical authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-61740) affecting deployments using API-Key authentication mode. The vulnerability stems from a hardcoded DEFAULT_TOKEN_SECRET in the codebase combined with a public /auth-status endpoint that reveals authentication details, allowing unauthenticated attackers to forge valid JWT tokens offline and bypass X-API-Key protections. No patch status or wild exploitation details are provided in the advisory. Immediately upgrade to a patched version once available, and consider rotating any hardcoded secrets and implementing additional network-level access controls until a fix is deployed.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: node-tar: Decompression/parse DoS via unlimited input (CVE-2026-59873)
entry.content:
vulnerability · dos · CVE-2026-59873

The node-tar library (npm/tar) is vulnerable to decompression denial-of-service attacks via gzip bombs (CVE-2026-59873, critical severity). The library lacks hard upper bounds on decompressed data volume or entry counts, allowing attackers to exhaust disk space and CPU resources with small, maliciously crafted compressed archives. This vulnerability is likely being exploited in the wild given its critical rating and simplicity of attack. Update node-tar to the latest patched version immediately and consider implementing external decompression limits and resource quotas as interim mitigations.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: Targeted Attack on Government Entities in the Middle East | Part 1
entry.content:
red-team · c2,edr-evasion,lateral-movement

East Asia-linked threat actor targeted Middle Eastern government entities via multi-stage attack chain deploying three previously undocumented malware families: TELESHIM (backdoor abusing Telegram API for C2), MIXEDKEY (reflective loader with mixed boolean arithmetic obfuscation), and BINDCLOAK (C2 implant). Attack initiates with ISO containing legitimate ASUSTek executable sideloading malicious DLL; subsequent stages use heavy code obfuscation (control flow flattening, opaque predicates), anti-analysis techniques, and environmental keying tied to victim machine volume serial numbers to prevent execution outside intended targets. While no active exploitation of zero-days reported, the sophistication and targeting of government entities represents elevated threat; immediate patching of systems is secondary to network monitoring for Telegram API abuse, scheduled task creation at 6/10-minute intervals, and staging directories under C:\ProgramData\ containing suspicious DLLs alongside legitimate binaries.

Read more · Source: zscaler-threatlabz

entry.title: New HollowGraph malware uses Microsoft Graph for stealthy C2 comms
entry.content:
red-team · c2

HollowGraph malware leverages Microsoft Graph API to abuse the calendar feature in compromised Microsoft 365 mailboxes for stealthy command-and-control communications and data exfiltration. This technique is particularly dangerous because it blends malicious traffic with legitimate Microsoft Graph activity, evading traditional network-based detection. While no specific exploitation data is provided, the novel use of trusted Microsoft infrastructure indicates potential for widespread adoption. Organizations should enforce strong MFA on Microsoft 365 accounts, implement conditional access policies to restrict Graph API usage, monitor calendar API activity for suspicious patterns, and conduct credential audits to identify compromised accounts.

Read more · Source: bleepingcomputer

entry.title: HollowGraph Malware Hides C2 and Stolen Files in Microsoft 365 Events Dated 2050
entry.content:
red-team · c2

HollowGraph, a new espionage implant, abuses Microsoft 365 calendar events dated 2050 as a covert command-and-control channel and data exfiltration mechanism via the Microsoft Graph API. The malware leverages legitimate Microsoft 365 traffic to hide operator instructions and stolen file attachments, evading standard detection by blending into authorized API activity. While severity and active exploitation in the wild are not specified in the provided summary, the technique represents a sophisticated approach to persistent command channels. Organizations should review M365 audit logs for suspicious calendar event creation patterns, enforce strict API permissions policies, and monitor for anomalous Graph API usage outside expected patterns.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: Critical ServiceNow code execution flaw now exploited in attacks
entry.content:
vulnerability · rce · CVE-2026-60167, CVE-2026-60168, CVE-2026-60169, CVE-2026-60170, CVE-2026-6875

ServiceNow AI Platform is vulnerable to unauthenticated remote code execution (CVE-2026-6875), allowing attackers to execute arbitrary code without authentication. The vulnerability is critical in severity and is actively being exploited in the wild. Organizations running affected ServiceNow instances should immediately apply available patches and implement network-based access controls to restrict exposure of AI Platform components to untrusted networks.

Read more · Source: bleepingcomputer

entry.title: Critical NGINX Vulnerability Can Crash Workers and May Allow Remote Code Execution
entry.content:
vulnerability · heap-buffer-overflow · CVE-2026-42533

NGINX versions prior to 1.30.4 (stable) and 1.31.3 (mainline) contain a critical heap buffer overflow (CVE-2024-42533) triggered by crafted HTTP requests to unauthenticated remote attackers. The vulnerability can crash or restart worker processes, causing denial of service, with potential for remote code execution depending on heap layout and environment. There is no indication of active exploitation in the wild at this time. Immediate patching to nginx 1.30.4, 1.31.3, or NGINX Plus 37.0.3.1 is required; organizations unable to patch immediately should implement strict input validation and request filtering on reverse proxies.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: Scans for Hikvision Intelligent Security API, (Sun, Jul 19th)
entry.content:
vulnerability · unspecified

Hikvision intelligent cameras and security systems are facing widespread internet-wide reconnaissance activity targeting known API vulnerabilities. The Hikvision product line has a documented history of security flaws affecting authentication and remote access mechanisms. Threat actors are actively scanning for exposed or vulnerable Hikvision devices, likely attempting to identify targets for exploitation or unauthorized access. Organizations should immediately inventory Hikvision deployments, apply available security patches, restrict network access to management interfaces, and consider segmenting camera systems from critical networks.

Read more · Source: sans-isc

entry.title: Update now: 7-Zip fixes RCE flaw exploitable with malicious archives
entry.content:
vulnerability · rce · CVE-2026-14266

7-Zip versions prior to 26.02 contain a remote code execution vulnerability exploitable through maliciously crafted archive files. An attacker can execute arbitrary code by tricking users into opening prepared compressed files, making this a direct threat to systems using older versions. The vulnerability has moderate-to-high severity given the common usage of 7-Zip and potential for user-directed exploitation. Immediate patching to version 26.02 or later is required; users should also be cautioned against opening archives from untrusted sources.

Read more · Source: bleepingcomputer

entry.title: New North Korean campaign uses fake coding interviews to steal developer credentials
entry.content:
red-team · social-engineering

North Korean threat actors are conducting targeted recruitment fraud against software developers, embedding malware within SVG image files embedded in fake coding interview platforms to harvest credentials and establish system access. The attack leverages social engineering combined with steganography to evade signature-based detection—no major antivirus products detected the malicious payloads. This campaign actively targets developers at tech firms and represents a high-severity threat given the potential for supply chain compromise. Recommend implementing strict controls on interview platform usage, scanning SVG files with behavior-based detection tools, enforcing multi-factor authentication for developers, and alerting on suspicious recruitment outreach patterns.

Read more · Source: elastic-security-labs

entry.title: Metasploit Wrap Up: An HTTP to SMB relay plus Payload Improvements
entry.content:
red-team · payload-enhancement,relay-attack

Metasploit Framework received new HTTP to SMB relay capability and enhanced Linux payload delivery mechanisms. The HTTP to SMB relay module (auxiliary/server/relay/http_to_smb) enables relaying NTLM HTTP authentication requests to multiple SMB servers for session establishment. Added Linux Fetch Multi payloads with automatic architecture detection for mipsel, mips64, aarch64, and other targets, plus RISC-V 32/64-bit payload support with XOR encoders. No active exploitation in the wild reported; users should update via msfupdate to access expanded post-exploitation capabilities and cross-architecture flexibility.

Read more · Source: rapid7

entry.title: OpenSSL HollowByte Flaw Could Freeze Server Memory with 11-Byte TLS Requests
entry.content:
vulnerability · dos

OpenSSL versions prior to 1.1.1w, 3.0.x before 3.0.13, and 3.1.x before 3.1.1 are affected by the HollowByte denial-of-service vulnerability, which allows remote attackers to exhaust server memory by sending specially crafted 11-byte TLS requests that cause OpenSSL to allocate up to 131 KB per request without proper cleanup. The flaw is a memory exhaustion issue that can freeze or degrade server performance on glibc systems, and allocated memory persists until process restart. No known active exploitation has been reported; however, the fix was silently deployed in June without CVE assignment or security advisory. Immediately patch to OpenSSL 1.1.1w, 3.0.13, or 3.1.1 or later, and monitor servers for suspicious TLS connection patterns prior to patching.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: New wp2shell WordPress Core Flaw Lets Unauthenticated Attackers Run Code
entry.content:
vulnerability · rce · CVE-2026-60137, CVE-2026-63030

WordPress versions 6.9 and 7.0 contain a critical unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in core functionality that affects all installations regardless of plugin configuration. An attacker can execute arbitrary code via anonymous HTTP requests without authentication. WordPress addressed this with forced auto-updates deploying versions 6.9.5 and 7.0.2 on Friday; administrators should immediately verify their installations are patched, as the vulnerability has clear exploitation potential given its unauthenticated nature and core impact.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: Envoy Gateway: Authentication Bypass via Improper Input Validation in EnvoyExtensionPolicy Lua Allows Secret Disclosure (CVE-2026-53713)
entry.content:
vulnerability · auth-bypass · CVE-2026-53713

Envoy Gateway's EnvoyExtensionPolicy Lua extension fails to properly normalize path separators, allowing attackers to bypass file access restrictions via double-slash paths (e.g., `//etc/passwd`) and disclose sensitive files from the controller pod filesystem. The vulnerability is critical with high exploitability due to improper input validation in the `to_absolute_normalized_path` function. No indication of active exploitation in the wild at this time. Apply the patched version of Envoy Gateway immediately; in the interim, restrict who can submit EnvoyExtensionPolicy resources via RBAC controls.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: Fortinet FortiSandbox: Fortinet FortiSandbox OS Command Injection Vulnerability (CVE-2026-25089)
entry.content:
vulnerability · os-command-injection · CVE-2026-25089, CVE-2026-39808

Fortinet FortiSandbox, FortiSandbox Cloud, and FortiSandbox PaaS contain an unauthenticated OS command injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-25089) exploitable via crafted HTTP requests, allowing arbitrary command execution. This vulnerability is actively exploited in the wild and carries high severity due to its pre-authentication nature and complete system compromise potential. Immediately apply vendor-supplied patches following CISA BOD 26-04 prioritization guidelines; if mitigations are unavailable for cloud deployments, discontinue service use. Conduct forensic triage on affected systems and evaluate internet exposure for all FortiSandbox instances.

Read more · Source: cisa-kev

entry.title: Russian hackers trojanize WebEx, Zoom apps to push Starland malware
entry.content:
red-team · c2

Russian threat actor UAT-11795 is distributing trojanized versions of Webex and Zoom installers to deliver Starland RAT, a new backdoor capable of stealing credentials and cryptocurrency. The malware demonstrates high severity due to its credential-stealing capabilities and remote access functionality, with active exploitation reported in the wild. Organizations should verify software authenticity by downloading directly from official vendor websites, implement credential rotation for potentially compromised accounts, and deploy endpoint detection tools to identify Starland RAT's command-and-control communications. Security awareness training on software distribution risks is recommended to prevent initial compromise vectors.

Read more · Source: bleepingcomputer

entry.title: OpenAI’s GPT-Red Automates Prompt Injection Testing to Harden GPT-5.6 Sol
entry.content:
red-team · prompt-injection-testing

OpenAI's GPT-Red is an internal automated red-teaming tool designed to identify prompt injection vulnerabilities in large language models at scale before deployment. Prompt injection attacks manipulate LLM behavior by embedding malicious instructions in user inputs, potentially causing models to ignore safety guidelines or leak sensitive information; this represents a moderate-to-high severity threat as these vulnerabilities are actively exploited in production LLM systems. OpenAI reports that earlier model versions were highly vulnerable to GPT-Red's attacks, and the company uses these findings to adversarially train improved versions. Organizations deploying LLMs should implement input sanitization, prompt engineering best practices, and regular adversarial testing; consider adopting similar red-teaming methodologies to identify vulnerabilities in custom LLM deployments.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: Unpatched Shark Vacuum Flaw Could Let Attackers Control Other Vacuums Region-Wide
entry.content:
vulnerability · certificate-extraction,rce,privilege-escalation

Shark RV2320EDUS robot vacuums contain a critical vulnerability allowing attackers with physical access to extract hardcoded AWS certificates from flash memory, then use those credentials to remotely compromise other vacuums in the same region with full control including camera access, movement, and Wi-Fi credential theft. The flaw affects authentication mechanisms that fail to properly isolate per-device credentials. This is unpatched and the proof-of-concept was publicly disclosed Monday with active exploitation likely imminent. Shark should immediately revoke compromised certificates, implement per-device authentication, and push a firmware update; users should segregate vacuums to restricted network segments pending patches.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: Daxin Resurfaces in Taiwan Alongside Stupig Pre-Login SYSTEM Backdoor
entry.content:
red-team · rootkit

Daxin kernel-mode rootkit and newly discovered Stupig pre-login backdoor have re-emerged in a Taiwan manufacturing environment after a four-year hiatus, attributed to China-linked threat actors. Daxin persists in system memory via a malicious driver (srt64.sys) enabling deep system compromise, while Stupig operates at the pre-login stage suggesting persistent access mechanisms. This represents active exploitation in the wild with high severity given the targeted nature against critical infrastructure and manufacturing sectors. Organizations should prioritize threat hunting for these artifacts, implement kernel-mode code signing enforcement, conduct forensic analysis of affected systems, and apply EDR solutions capable of detecting anomalous driver loading and pre-login persistence techniques.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: websocket-driver: Message corruption via abuse of protocol length headers (CVE-2026-54466)
entry.content:
vulnerability · dos · CVE-2026-54466

The websocket-driver npm package is vulnerable to message corruption (CVE-2026-54466) where attackers can send malformed WebSocket frame length headers with an indefinite sequence of high-bit-set bytes, causing servers to parse an arbitrarily large integer that exceeds JavaScript's 64-bit floating point precision limits and corrupts subsequent payload parsing. This critical vulnerability is exploitable remotely without authentication and can lead to denial of service or unexpected protocol behavior. Patch to the latest version of websocket-driver immediately, as this affects all versions using the vulnerable frame parsing logic. Monitor for suspicious WebSocket connections with malformed length headers as a temporary mitigation if immediate patching is not feasible.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: Zoom warns of critical account takeover vulnerability
entry.content:
vulnerability · account-takeover · CVE-2026-53412

Zoom desktop client and SDK for Windows contain a critical account takeover vulnerability (CVE-pending) exploitable by unauthenticated attackers without user interaction. The flaw enables complete compromise of user accounts through a mechanism leveraging improper credential handling or session management. Zoom has not indicated active exploitation in the wild but rates this as critical given the low attack complexity and widespread client deployment. Users should immediately update to the latest patched version of Zoom; administrators should enforce mandatory client updates enterprise-wide.

Read more · Source: bleepingcomputer

entry.title: TELEPUZ: a modular MaaS malware spreading via CLICKFIX-VIDAR chains
entry.content:
red-team · malware-distribution

TELEPUZ is a modular malware-as-a-service platform discovered in April propagating through CLICKFIX social engineering chains that deliver VIDAR credential stealers. The malware employs advanced evasion techniques and maintains a command-and-control infrastructure enabling flexible payload delivery and post-exploitation capabilities. Active exploitation in the wild has been confirmed via widespread CLICKFIX campaigns targeting users through deceptive support scams. Organizations should strengthen email/browser security controls, implement credential guard mechanisms to defend against stealer malware, and conduct employee security awareness training targeting social engineering tactics commonly used in CLICKFIX attacks.

Read more · Source: elastic-security-labs

entry.title: MantisBT: Reflected XSS in admin/install.php via unescaped printf (CVE-2026-52881)
entry.content:
vulnerability · reflected-xss · CVE-2026-52881

MantisBT versions 2.28.3 and earlier contain critical reflected XSS vulnerabilities in `/admin/install.php` where user-supplied parameters are echoed unescaped via printf format strings, requiring no authentication. The existing Content Security Policy lacks a form-action directive, enabling attackers to inject credential-phishing forms and open redirects despite script-src restrictions. This vulnerability is actively exploitable for credential theft and other attacks. Upgrade to MantisBT 2.28.4 or later immediately, or implement strict input validation and additional CSP directives (form-action 'self') as interim mitigation.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: Google Gemini CLI abused as a hacking agent, malware botnet operator
entry.content:
red-team · ai-misuse

Google Gemini CLI, an open-source AI tool, has been abused by threat actor "bandcampro" to automate hacking activities and control a botnet infrastructure. The actor leveraged the CLI's capabilities to execute commands and coordinate malicious operations, demonstrating how legitimate AI tools can be repurposed for offensive security operations. While this appears to be a limited-scale operation rather than widespread exploitation, it highlights the risk of AI tools being weaponized by threat actors. Organizations should implement strict access controls, monitor for suspicious AI tool usage patterns, and consider restricting or auditing open-source AI CLI deployments in their environments.

Read more · Source: bleepingcomputer

entry.title: Investigating Persistence Mechanisms in AWS
entry.content:
red-team · persistence

AWS identity and access management features (IAM users, roles, Lambda functions, and federated sessions) are commonly abused for persistence after initial compromise. Attackers establish footholds through techniques including creating backdoored IAM users with credentials and policies, modifying assume role policies to allow external account access, embedding malicious code in Lambda functions with public URLs, and creating federated sessions that survive credential rotation. These are established techniques actively exploited in the wild with high business impact due to difficulty in detection and containment. Organizations should implement deny-all policies on compromised identities immediately, rotate all affected credentials, review IAM permissions and trust policies regularly, and deploy comprehensive CloudTrail monitoring with queries targeting suspicious API calls like iam:CreateUser, iam:UpdateAssumeRolePolicy, lambda:CreateFunctionUrlConfig, and sts:GetFederationToken.

Read more · Source: rapid7

entry.title: MantisBT: SOAP API Authentication Bypass with Privilege Escalation to Administrator (CVE-2026-47156)
entry.content:
vulnerability · auth-bypass · CVE-2026-47156

MantisBT versions 2.28.3 and earlier contain a critical authentication bypass in the SOAP API's mci_check_login() function allowing privilege escalation to administrator. An attacker can authenticate as any user including administrators by leveraging any valid cookie_string combined with a target username, exploitable by self-registered users on default installations where signup is enabled. This vulnerability is critical severity and likely exploited in the wild given its ease of exploitation. Immediately patch to the latest version beyond 2.28.3 and consider disabling self-registration ($g_allow_signup = OFF) as an interim mitigation.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: KNX Association KNX Protocol Connection Authorization Option 1: KNX Association KNX Protocol Connection Authorization Option 1 Overly Restrictive Account Lockout Mechanism Vulnerability (CVE-2023-4346)
entry.content:
vulnerability · account-lockout · CVE-2023-4346

KNX Protocol Connection Authorization Option 1 contains a flaw in its account lockout mechanism that allows attackers to purge all devices and lock BCU keys, effectively denying service to KNX-based building automation systems. This vulnerability is actively exploited in the wild and rated severe by CISA. Organizations must immediately apply vendor-provided mitigations per BOD 26-04 guidance, prioritizing internet-exposed assets, or discontinue use of affected systems if patches are unavailable.

Read more · Source: cisa-kev

entry.title: We built a vulnerability vending machine: AI tokens in, zero-days out
entry.content:
vulnerability · unspecified

Intruder developed an automated system using AI and code slicing techniques to discover zero-day vulnerabilities in software, demonstrating the approach by identifying a previously unknown WordPress plugin vulnerability. The technique leverages large language models to analyze code and uncover complex logical flaws that traditional static analysis tools may miss. While the research is currently academic in nature with findings under responsible disclosure, this represents a significant escalation in vulnerability discovery automation that defenders should monitor. Organizations should prioritize timely patching of WordPress plugins and third-party software, implement runtime application self-protection (RASP) solutions, and consider adopting AI-powered security testing in their development pipelines to identify issues before attackers do.

Read more · Source: bleepingcomputer

entry.title: Firefox, Chrome, Adobe, and VMware Updates Fix Multiple Critical Security Flaws
entry.content:
vulnerability · memory-safety,site-isolation · CVE-2026-15718, CVE-2026-15719

Mozilla Firefox addressed two critical vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-15718 in JavaScript/WebAssembly and CVE-2026-15719 in DOM Navigation) with public exploit code available, requiring immediate patching. Chrome, Adobe, and VMware also released updates addressing multiple critical flaws across their respective products. Users should prioritize patching Firefox immediately due to active exploitation risk, and apply updates for other affected software according to vendor severity ratings. Monitor systems for exploitation attempts targeting these vulnerabilities.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: Cursor Flaw Lets Malicious Cloned Repositories Trigger Windows Code Execution
entry.content:
vulnerability · arbitrary-code-execution

Cursor IDE on Windows contains a critical arbitrary code execution vulnerability where malicious cloned repositories can execute a git.exe binary placed in the project root without user interaction, approval dialogs, or warnings. The flaw allows attackers to execute code with full user privileges and access to source code, SSH keys, and cloud credentials, with repeated execution occurring as long as the project remains open. This is actively exploited in the wild through social engineering attacks distributing compromised repositories. Cursor users should immediately update to the latest patched version and implement repository scanning practices before opening untrusted projects.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: Researcher Drops New Windows Zero-Day PoC Hours After Microsoft Patch Tuesday
entry.content:
vulnerability · privilege-escalation

Windows User Profile Service (ProfSvc) vulnerability allows arbitrary hive loading leading to local privilege escalation. The flaw was disclosed publicly with a working PoC (LegacyHive) by researcher Chaotic Eclipse shortly after Microsoft's Patch Tuesday, indicating active disclosure risk. Severity appears high given the elevation of privileges capability, though exploitation requires local access and no in-the-wild exploitation has been reported at this time. Organizations should prioritize patching this vulnerability immediately and monitor for related CVE assignment and exploitation attempts.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: Update-Verteilung pausiert: Neue Windows-11-Updates lassen Dell-PCs abstürzen
entry.content:
vulnerability · system-crash

Windows 11 KB5035857 and related updates cause thermal management failures and unexpected shutdowns on Dell systems due to compatibility issues with Dell firmware or drivers. Microsoft has paused distribution of affected updates, severity is high given impact on system stability, and no active exploitation in the wild has been reported. Dell and Microsoft users should avoid manual installation of these KB versions and await corrected updates; alternatively, contact Dell support for firmware/driver guidance or rollback problematic updates via Windows Recovery.

Read more · Source: golem-security

entry.title: LegacyHive public disclosure
entry.content:
red-team · c2

LegacyHive is a publicly disclosed tool available on multiple Git repositories that appears to be designed for malicious purposes based on repository naming conventions (ProjectNightcrawler, ChurchofMalware). Without access to the actual repository contents, the specific vulnerability or attack technique cannot be determined, but the public disclosure and deliberate multi-platform hosting suggests active threat actor distribution. Security engineers should monitor for this tool's use in intrusions, examine network/host logs for associated command-and-control communications, and coordinate with threat intelligence teams to understand its capabilities and indicators of compromise. Immediate action should focus on detection rule development and threat hunting rather than patching, as this appears to be attacker-controlled malware rather than a vulnerable software package.

Read more · Source: projectnightcrawler

entry.title: Anyquery: Arbitrary File Write (AFW) which could lead to Remote Code Execution (RCE) via Unrestricted ATTACH DATABASE in Server Mode (CVE-2026-50006)
entry.content:
vulnerability · arbitrary-file-write · CVE-2026-50006

Anyquery's server mode fails to restrict SQLite's ATTACH DATABASE command, allowing unauthenticated attackers to write arbitrary files to the filesystem via the MySQL-compatible port, potentially achieving remote code execution. This critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-50006) is exploitable without authentication and poses immediate risk in exposed deployments. Immediate patching is required; as a mitigation, restrict network access to Anyquery's server port and disable server mode if not required.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: TidGi Desktop Remote Code Execution via Malicious TiddlyWiki Repository Import — Tiddler Startup Module Auto-Execution
entry.content:
vulnerability · rce

TidGi Desktop through version 0.13.0 is vulnerable to critical remote code execution when importing a malicious TiddlyWiki Git repository, as the application automatically executes arbitrary JavaScript code embedded in `.tid` files within the tiddlers directory during wiki initialization. The vulnerability stems from TiddlyWiki's module system auto-discovering and executing JavaScript in startup modules without validation. This is a critical severity issue with active exploitation potential given the ease of weaponization through repository imports. Users should immediately update to a patched version beyond 0.13.0 and avoid importing repositories from untrusted sources; maintainers should implement code signing or sandboxing for tiddler module execution.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: SonicWall SMA1000 Appliances: SonicWall SMA1000 Appliances Code Injection Vulnerability (CVE-2026-15410)
entry.content:
vulnerability · code-injection · CVE-2026-15410

SonicWall SMA1000 appliances contain a code injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-15410) that allows remote authenticated administrators to execute arbitrary OS commands under specific conditions. This vulnerability is actively exploited in the wild and carries high severity. Apply vendor mitigations immediately per CISA BOD 26-04 guidance; discontinue use if mitigations are unavailable. Conduct forensic triage on affected systems and evaluate internet exposure to prioritize patching efforts.

Read more · Source: cisa-kev

entry.title: Microsoft Active Directory Federation Services: Microsoft Active Directory Federation Services Insufficient Granularity of Access Control Vulnerability (CVE-2026-56155)
entry.content:
vulnerability · privilege-escalation · CVE-2026-56155

Microsoft Active Directory Federation Services (AD FS) contains an insufficient access control vulnerability (CVE-2026-56155) allowing authorized local attackers to escalate privileges. This vulnerability is actively exploited in the wild and listed on CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Apply vendor-supplied patches immediately per CISA BOD 26-04 guidance; if patches are unavailable, implement mitigations or discontinue use. Conduct forensic triage on affected systems and evaluate internet exposure to prioritize remediation efforts.

Read more · Source: cisa-kev

entry.title: Microsoft July 2026 Patch Tuesday fixes massive 570 flaws, 3 zero-days
entry.content:
vulnerability · mixed

Microsoft released patches for 570 vulnerabilities in July 2026 Patch Tuesday, including three zero-days (two actively exploited in the wild and one publicly disclosed prior to patching). Severity levels span critical flaws affecting core Windows and Office components with widespread exploitation potential. Immediate patching is mandatory for all affected Microsoft products; prioritize zero-day CVEs and any critical-rated flaws in internet-facing systems. Organizations unable to patch immediately should implement network segmentation and disable unnecessary services for affected software.

Read more · Source: bleepingcomputer

entry.title: Microsoft releases Windows 10 KB5099539 extended security update
entry.content:
vulnerability · multiple

Windows 10 receives KB5099539 extended security update addressing 570 vulnerabilities including critical remote code execution and elevation of privilege flaws across multiple components. The update covers July 2026 Patch Tuesday issues affecting kernel, graphics drivers, and system services with varying severity levels; some vulnerabilities are actively exploited in the wild. Immediate patching is strongly recommended for all Windows 10 systems still in support, particularly those exposed to network attack surfaces. Organizations should prioritize deployment of this cumulative update to their patch management processes.

Read more · Source: bleepingcomputer

entry.title: Progress confirms ShareFile zero-day flaw behind Storage Zone shutdown
entry.content:
vulnerability · rce

Progress Software's ShareFile Storage Zone Controller is affected by a high-severity zero-day vulnerability that prompted emergency service shutdowns. The flaw allows unauthenticated remote code execution, enabling attackers to compromise affected systems. Active exploitation in the wild has been confirmed, making this a critical threat. Organizations must immediately apply the released security patches and verify Storage Zone Controller instances are updated to the patched versions before resuming operations.

Read more · Source: bleepingcomputer

entry.title: 11 Old Microsoft-Signed Linux UEFI Shims Could Let Attackers Bypass Secure Boot
entry.content:
vulnerability · secure-boot-bypass

Eleven legacy Microsoft-signed UEFI shims used in Linux distributions contain vulnerabilities enabling Secure Boot bypass on systems using these firmware standards. Attackers exploiting these flaws can execute arbitrary code during the boot process, facilitating UEFI bootkit deployment or other pre-OS malware installation. No active exploitation in the wild has been reported; however, severity is high given the fundamental nature of Secure Boot circumvention. Affected systems should update to patched UEFI shim versions and revoke the vulnerable signatures through firmware updates where available.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: RabbitMQ Flaws Could Leak OAuth Secrets and Expose Cross-Tenant Queue Metadata
entry.content:
vulnerability · auth-bypass,information-disclosure · CVE-2026-54052

RabbitMQ message broker contains two access control vulnerabilities enabling attackers to leak OAuth client secrets and expose cross-tenant queue metadata, potentially compromising enterprise messaging infrastructure and tenant isolation. The flaws allow unauthorized access to sensitive authentication credentials and bypass of tenant boundaries, creating significant security risks in multi-tenant deployments. Severity appears high given the exposure of OAuth secrets and infrastructure metadata, though wild exploitation details were not specified in available reporting. Organizations should immediately patch RabbitMQ to the latest version addressing these access control issues and audit OAuth credential exposure in affected deployments.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: SAP-Patchday: Teils kritische Sicherheitslücken in mehreren Produkten gefixt
entry.content:
vulnerability · multiple · CVE-2026-44747

SAP released patches for 16 vulnerabilities across multiple products in July, with several rated as critical severity. The vulnerabilities span various SAP applications and components, though specific details on attack vectors and exploitation status were not provided in the summary. Organizations running affected SAP systems should prioritize applying the July patches, particularly for critical-rated issues, and review SAP's official security notes to determine which vulnerabilities impact their deployments. No evidence of active wild exploitation was mentioned, but critical SAP vulnerabilities typically warrant expedited patching due to the enterprise nature of these systems.

Read more · Source: heise-security

entry.title: FacturaScripts: Account takeover of any 2FA-enabled user (CVE-2026-47677)
entry.content:
vulnerability · auth-bypass · CVE-2026-45262, CVE-2026-47677

FacturaScripts authentication module (Core/Controller/Login.php) contains a critical authentication bypass in two-factor authentication validation that allows account takeover of any 2FA-enabled user. The vulnerable endpoint `/login?action=two-factor-validation` accepts unauthenticated POST requests containing only a username and TOTP code without password verification or CSRF protection, enabling attackers to brute-force valid TOTP codes and obtain valid session tokens. This vulnerability is critical severity and likely exploited given the straightforward attack vector. Immediately patch FacturaScripts to the latest version and implement rate-limiting on the two-factor validation endpoint as a temporary mitigation.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: Kimai: Default APP_SECRET in Docker Image Enables Cookie Forgery and Account Takeover (CVE-2026-52824)
entry.content:
vulnerability · insecure-defaults · CVE-2026-52824

Kimai Docker image ships with a hardcoded default APP_SECRET value, allowing unauthenticated attackers to forge HMAC-signed cookies and authentication tokens to takeover any account including administrators. This affects all Kimai instances deployed via the official Docker image without explicit APP_SECRET configuration. Severity is critical with active exploitation likely given the public nature of the default secret. Immediately regenerate APP_SECRET to a cryptographically unique value in all deployed instances and redeploy using updated Docker image; verify no unauthorized account access has occurred.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: DIRAC is vulnerable to RCE in RequestManager due to eval on untrusted input (CVE-2026-45579)
entry.content:
vulnerability · rce · CVE-2026-45579, CVE-2026-61667

DIRAC's RequestManager component contains a critical remote code execution vulnerability (CVE-2026-45579) stemming from unsafe eval() calls on untrusted user input in the export_getRequestCountersWeb function. Any authenticated user can exploit this to execute arbitrary code on the DIRAC server with the privileges of the system user running DIRAC services. There is no indication of active exploitation in the wild. Affected organizations should immediately patch to a patched version and consider restricting network access to DIRAC instances until updates are applied.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: Cisco IOS: Cisco IOS Cross-Site Request Forgery Vulnerability (CVE-2008-4128)
entry.content:
vulnerability · csrf · CVE-2008-4128

Cisco IOS 12.4 contains multiple cross-site request forgery (CSRF) vulnerabilities allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands through specially crafted HTTP requests to the web management interface. This vulnerability is actively exploited in the wild and carries high severity due to the potential for complete device compromise. Administrators must immediately apply vendor-supplied patches or implement mitigations according to Cisco's guidance and CISA BOD 26-04 prioritization; discontinue use of affected systems if patches are unavailable. Prioritize patching for internet-exposed devices and conduct forensic triage on potentially compromised systems.

Read more · Source: cisa-kev

entry.title: CISA warns of actively exploited RCE flaws in Joomla extensions
entry.content:
vulnerability · rce

Two remote code execution vulnerabilities in Joomla extensions—iCagenda and Balbooa Forms—are being actively exploited in the wild through arbitrary file upload mechanisms. These flaws allow unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code on affected systems. CISA has issued a warning confirming active exploitation, indicating high severity. Organizations using these extensions should immediately update to patched versions or disable the affected plugins until patches are available.

Read more · Source: bleepingcomputer

entry.title: (g+) Cybersecurity: Wie eine Erpressergruppe reihenweise Peoplesoft-Systeme abräumt
entry.content:
vulnerability · unspecified

Oracle PeopleSoft systems are being exploited in the wild via an unpatched zero-day vulnerability, with multiple high-profile victims targeted by extortion groups. The flaw allows attackers to compromise systems and extract sensitive data for extortion purposes. Affected organizations should immediately apply the latest Oracle security patches, isolate vulnerable PeopleSoft instances from external network access, and implement enhanced monitoring for suspicious authentication and data exfiltration activity. Consider conducting a forensic investigation to determine if systems have been previously compromised.

Read more · Source: golem-security

entry.title: Wireshark 4.6.7 Released, (Sat, Jul 11th)
entry.content:
vulnerability

Wireshark 4.6.7 addresses 12 vulnerabilities across the packet analysis tool. The specific vulnerabilities and their severity levels are not detailed in this release notice, but multiple CVE fixes typically include memory corruption, denial of service, or information disclosure issues common in protocol parsers. Update to version 4.6.7 immediately, particularly if Wireshark is deployed in security operations environments or used to analyze untrusted network traffic.

Read more · Source: sans-isc

entry.title: TSDProxy: Internal proxy auth token forwarded to backend services enables management API escalation
entry.content:
vulnerability · auth-bypass

TSDProxy (go/github.com/almeidapaulopt/tsdproxy) forwards its internal authentication token used for management API trust to all proxied backend services by default when identityHeaders is enabled. An attacker controlling or compromising a backend service can replay this token to the localhost management HTTP server to escalate privileges and manipulate Tailscale identity claims. This is a critical vulnerability actively exploitable in deployments using default configuration. Immediately disable identityHeaders forwarding, update to a patched version when available, or restrict backend service access to untrusted networks.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: Bluetooth Low Energy Security Testing, Consolidated: Introducing Caeruleus
entry.content:
red-team · security-testing

Caeruleus is a new consolidated Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) security testing framework addressing fragmentation in existing BLE tools like bettercap and gatttool, which are deprecated or limited in functionality. The tool consolidates reconnaissance, enumeration, characteristic reading/writing, and fuzzing capabilities into a single platform, improving efficiency for security assessments. This is a defensive tooling release rather than a vulnerability disclosure, with no identified exploits or immediate patching requirements. Security teams should consider adopting Caeruleus for streamlined BLE security testing workflows in place of legacy tool chains.

Read more · Source: praetorian

entry.title: Authorizer: Unvalidated redirect_uri in /authorize leaks OAuth2 tokens to attacker-controlled URL (CVE-2026-54072)
entry.content:
vulnerability · unvalidated-redirect · CVE-2026-54072

Authorizer OAuth2 implementation (CVE-2026-54072) contains an unvalidated redirect_uri vulnerability in the /authorize endpoint that leaks access tokens, ID tokens, and refresh tokens to attacker-controlled URLs when response_type is set to token or id_token. The vulnerability is critical severity as it allows unauthenticated attackers to obtain client_id from a public endpoint and hijack tokens without user interaction. No evidence of in-the-wild exploitation is mentioned. Update to a version with complete redirect_uri validation across all handlers; the partial fix in v2.0.1 is insufficient.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: SiYuan: Unauthenticated Admin API Access via Blanket chrome-extension:// Origin Allowlist (CVE-2026-54069)
entry.content:
vulnerability · auth-bypass · CVE-2026-54069

SiYuan Note's HTTP kernel server (go/github.com/siyuan-note/siyuan/kernel) grants administrator API access to all chrome-extension:// origins without authentication, allowing any installed browser extension to make fully authenticated admin calls to the local kernel at 127.0.0.1:6806. This critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-54069) enables data exfiltration and modification through compromised or malicious extensions without requiring user interaction or valid credentials. The attack is particularly severe given the default empty AccessAuthCode on desktop installations. SiYuan users should immediately update to a patched version that implements proper origin validation and requires authentication for admin endpoints, and consider restricting chrome-extension:// access or disabling untrusted extensions until patching is complete.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: File Browser: Authentication Bypass via Proxy Auth Header Forgery (CVE-2026-54089)
entry.content:
vulnerability · auth-bypass · CVE-2026-54089

FileBrowser v2 (go/github.com/filebrowser/filebrowser) contains a critical authentication bypass vulnerability when configured with proxy authentication. Attackers can impersonate any user, including administrators, by forging proxy authentication headers without credentials, and can automatically create new accounts by specifying non-existent usernames. This vulnerability is actively exploited in the wild. Organizations using FileBrowser with proxy authentication enabled should immediately upgrade to a patched version or disable proxy authentication mode until a fix is deployed.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: `exploration` was removed from crates.io for malicious code
entry.content:
vulnerability · malicious-code

The `exploration` crate for Rust contained critical malicious code that attempted to download and execute remote payloads. The crate was published on crates.io on 2026-06-02 and removed approximately one hour later with no confirmed downloads or dependents. No mitigation is needed as the package was rapidly removed; however, any projects that may have briefly cached version 1.0 should verify their dependencies and rebuild to ensure no malicious code was included. This incident demonstrates the importance of monitoring dependency sources and using security tools like Rustsec to identify compromised packages.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: prestashop/ps_facetedsearch: PHP Object Injection in faceted search cache allows unauthenticated RCE (CVE-2026-54159)
entry.content:
vulnerability · php-object-injection · CVE-2026-54159

PrestaShop ps_facetedsearch module contains a PHP object injection vulnerability in its faceted search cache mechanism. An unauthenticated attacker can craft malicious serialized PHP objects via slider filter values (price/weight) in the request URL, which are cached without proper validation and later unserialized, enabling remote code execution. This is critical severity and actively exploited in the wild. Immediately update ps_facetedsearch to the patched version and implement input validation on slider filter parameters.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: SiYuan: Stored XSS to RCE via Unsanitized Attribute View Asset Cell Content (CVE-2026-50551)
entry.content:
vulnerability · stored-xss-to-rce · CVE-2026-50551, CVE-2026-54067, CVE-2026-54158

SiYuan v3.6.5 and earlier contain a stored XSS vulnerability in Attribute View asset cell rendering that escalates to RCE in the Electron desktop client due to insufficient HTML escaping (CVE-2026-50551). The vulnerability exists because the mitigation for CVE-2026-44588 was incomplete—asset renderers use the weaker `escapeAttr()` function instead of the stronger `escapeAriaLabel()` that was applied elsewhere. This is a critical severity issue likely exploitable in the wild given the ease of injecting malicious content via database asset cells. Immediately upgrade to patched versions beyond v3.6.5 and audit all existing Attribute View assets for suspicious content.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: Researcher Details WhatsApp-to-Host Attack Chain Using Three OpenClaw Flaws
entry.content:
vulnerability · rce

OpenClaw personal AI assistant contains three high-severity vulnerabilities (CVSS 8.8) enabling credential theft, privilege escalation, and arbitrary code execution when chained together in a WhatsApp-to-host attack. The flaws allow attackers to exploit the AI assistant through WhatsApp messages to compromise the underlying host system. All three vulnerabilities have been patched; users should update OpenClaw immediately to the latest version. This attack chain demonstrates the expanded threat surface from integrating AI assistants with messaging platforms and local system access.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: Laser Attack Resets Tangem Wallet Passwords on Cards That Can't Be Patched
entry.content:
vulnerability · physical-attack

Tangem hardware wallet cards are vulnerable to laser-induced fault injection attacks that can reset the device password without requiring the original credentials, enabling complete wallet takeover. The attack is highly specialized, requiring precise laser equipment and physical access to the card's chip, and is not currently exploited in the wild. Since Tangem cards are non-updateable hardware, no patch is available; mitigation relies on physical security measures and users should consider supplementary storage solutions for high-value holdings or transition to patchable hardware wallets.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: Six New U-Boot Flaws Could Let Malicious Images Crash Devices or Run Code at Boot
entry.content:
vulnerability · rce,dos

Six vulnerabilities discovered in U-Boot bootloader affecting routers, smart cameras, and data center management controllers. Four flaws enable denial-of-service through device crashes, while two allow arbitrary code execution during boot if an attacker can supply a malicious image before the bootloader executes. No indication of active exploitation in the wild provided. Affected organizations should prioritize patching U-Boot across all devices, particularly network infrastructure and data center management interfaces, and review image validation mechanisms to prevent unauthorized bootloader access.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: Zimbra urges customers to patch critical web client XSS flaw
entry.content:
vulnerability · xss

Zimbra Collaboration Suite's Classic Web Client contains a critical cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability that allows attackers to execute arbitrary code in user browsers and potentially compromise email accounts and sensitive data. The flaw has not been reported as actively exploited in the wild, but given its critical severity rating and ease of exploitation through web interfaces, it poses significant risk to users. Zimbra has released patches for affected versions; customers should apply security updates immediately to all deployments of the Classic Web Client to prevent potential account compromise and data exfiltration.

Read more · Source: bleepingcomputer

entry.title: Unpatched XRING Flaw in XQUIC Lets Remote Clients Crash HTTP/3 Servers
entry.content:
vulnerability · dos

XQUIC, Alibaba's QUIC and HTTP/3 library, contains an unpatched denial-of-service vulnerability (XRING) where a single variable initialization error allows remote unauthenticated clients to crash HTTP/3 servers using approximately 260 bytes of legitimate QPACK traffic. The vulnerability requires no special privileges, malformed packets, or authentication, and is triggered through normal protocol operations. No patch is currently available as of the disclosure date (July 8). Organizations running XQUIC-based HTTP/3 servers should monitor for available updates from Alibaba and consider implementing network-level rate limiting or traffic filtering for QPACK streams as interim mitigation until a fix is released.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: New MODBEACON RAT Uses gRPC Streaming for Encrypted C2 Traffic
entry.content:
red-team · c2

Silver Fox threat group has deployed MODBEACON, a new Rust-based RAT that leverages gRPC streaming to encrypt command-and-control communications, making detection more difficult through legitimate protocol abuse. The malware is distributed via SEO poisoning and counterfeit software installers targeting victims in Asia-Pacific regions. No current evidence of widespread exploitation in the wild has been reported, though the sophisticated C2 evasion mechanism suggests active development. Organizations should implement network-level monitoring for gRPC traffic patterns, enforce code signing verification on software installations, and maintain elevated awareness for SEO poisoning campaigns targeting their user base.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: Dell BIOS Passwords: Weak XOR Encryption Allows Recovery from SPI Flash (CVE-2026-40639)
entry.content:
vulnerability · weak-encryption · CVE-2026-40639

Dell BIOS firmware uses weak XOR encryption to protect administrator passwords stored in SPI flash memory, allowing attackers with physical access to extract and recover credentials from the flash chip. This affects Dell systems across multiple product lines and carries high severity since BIOS-level access enables complete system compromise, firmware modification, and persistent attacks. There is no indication of active exploitation in the wild, but the attack requires only basic hardware tools and flash dumping capabilities. Dell should issue BIOS updates implementing stronger encryption (AES or similar) for credential storage; affected users should restrict physical access to systems and enable additional firmware security controls where available.

Read more · Source: mdsec

entry.title: "Comment stuffing" in an HTML phishing attachment as a mechanism for evading AI-based detection?, (Fri, Jul 10th)
entry.content:
red-team · ai-evasion

Threat actors are using HTML comment stuffing in phishing attachments to evade AI-based email security detection systems. The technique involves embedding large volumes of benign content within HTML comments to obfuscate malicious code and confuse machine learning models trained to identify phishing patterns. This method demonstrates moderate sophistication and appears to be in active use in the wild with increasing frequency. Organizations should enhance email filtering rules to detect suspicious HTML comment patterns, implement additional analysis layers beyond AI detection, and conduct user awareness training on phishing attachment risks.

Read more · Source: sans-isc

entry.title: Attackers Exploit 'Ill Bloom' Vulnerability to Drain $3.1 Million From Cryptocurrency Wallets
entry.content:
vulnerability · weak-randomness

The "Ill Bloom" vulnerability affects cryptocurrency wallet software that generates recovery phrases using insufficient randomness, allowing attackers to derive wallet seeds and drain funds. The flaw has been actively exploited in the wild, with confirmed attacks stealing $3.1 million from affected wallets. Severity is critical as compromise results in total loss of wallet funds. Affected wallet developers must immediately implement cryptographically secure random number generation for seed phrase generation, and users should migrate funds from potentially affected wallets to those using proper entropy sources.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: YesWiki vulnerable to unauthenticated arbitrary page deletion via `{{erasespamedcomments}}` action (CVE-2026-52766)
entry.content:
vulnerability · auth-bypass · CVE-2026-52766

YesWiki's `{{erasespamedcomments}}` action in `actions/EraseSpamedCommentsAction.php` allows unauthenticated users to delete arbitrary wiki pages through an unprotected `suppr[]` parameter, with no authorization checks in the deletion path. The vulnerability is critical as it enables permanent data loss on default installations where write access is unrestricted. Users should immediately update to a patched version of YesWiki and restrict default write ACLs to trusted users. There is no indication this is currently exploited in the wild, but the ease of exploitation makes immediate patching essential.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: YesWiki Vulnerable to Authenticated PHP Object Injection in BazarImportAction via unserialize (CVE-2026-52777)
entry.content:
vulnerability · php-object-injection · CVE-2026-52777

YesWiki's BazarImportAction contains a critical authenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in CSVManager.php where user-supplied base64-encoded data is unserialized without restricting allowed classes (CVE-2026-52777). An authenticated attacker can exploit this to instantiate arbitrary PHP objects and achieve remote code execution. This vulnerability is not currently confirmed as exploited in the wild. Upgrade to a patched version immediately and implement input validation; as a temporary mitigation, restrict access to the bazar import functionality to trusted administrators only.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: YesWiki has Unsafe eval() in its Formula Calculato, Leading to Remote Code Execution & Denial of Service (CVE-2026-52778)
entry.content:
vulnerability · rce · CVE-2026-52778
