YesWiki's Bazar form field calculator (CalcField.php) is vulnerable to remote code execution and denial of service due to unsafe eval() execution of user-supplied mathematical formulas. The application's attempted sanitization via complex recursive regular expressions is bypassable, and the regex itself is susceptible to ReDoS attacks causing server crashes. This is a critical vulnerability likely exploitable in the wild given eval() usage patterns. Immediately update YesWiki to the latest patched version and consider disabling the calculator feature until patching is complete; do not rely on the existing sanitization as security.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: New GigaWiper Windows Backdoor Bundles Disk Wiping, Fake Ransomware, and Spyware
entry.content:
red-team · backdoor

GigaWiper is a Windows backdoor combining multiple destructive capabilities—disk wiping, drive overwriting, and fake ransomware with unrecoverable encryption—delivered as selectable operator commands. The threat demonstrates high severity with destructive intent, though Microsoft's analysis does not confirm active exploitation in the wild. Organizations should prioritize patching Windows systems and implementing defense-in-depth controls including EDR solutions, network segmentation, and restricted administrative access to limit backdoor deployment impact. Monitor for GigaWiper indicators of compromise and suspicious command execution patterns consistent with destructive malware activity.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: GodDamn Ransomware Uses PoisonX Driver to Disable Endpoint Defenses
entry.content:
red-team · edr-evasion

GodDamn ransomware, a Beast ransomware rebrand first observed in May 2026, leverages the PoisonX kernel driver to disable endpoint security solutions before encryption. This represents a high-severity threat exploited actively in the wild, combining defense evasion with ransomware deployment capabilities. Organizations should prioritize patching endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions, implement driver signature enforcement, and maintain immutable backups. Additional mitigation includes blocking unsigned kernel driver execution and monitoring for PoisonX driver IOCs across network infrastructure.

Read more · Source: thehackernews

entry.title: Update gegen Rogueplanet: Microsoft reagiert auf gefährlichen Defender-Exploit
entry.content:
vulnerability · privilege-escalation · CVE-2026-50656

Microsoft Defender contains a privilege escalation vulnerability exploited by the Rogueplanet exploit, which grants attackers elevated system rights on Windows. The vulnerability is actively exploited in the wild. Microsoft has released a security update to patch the flaw. All Windows users should apply this Defender update immediately as the exploit poses high severity risk.

Read more · Source: golem-security

entry.title: Nuclio: Unsanitized cron trigger event headers/body injected into CronJob shell command leads to persistent RCE (CVE-2026-52831)
entry.content:
vulnerability · command-injection · CVE-2026-52831, CVE-2026-54088

Nuclio container orchestration framework is vulnerable to persistent remote code execution through unsanitized cron trigger event headers and body parameters that are directly injected into Kubernetes CronJob shell commands (CVE-2026-52831, critical severity). An attacker can craft malicious header keys or event bodies containing shell metacharacters to break out of the intended quoting context and execute arbitrary commands with the privileges of the Nuclio controller. There is no indication of active exploitation in the wild, but the critical severity and ease of exploitation warrant immediate patching to the latest Nuclio version with proper input sanitization applied to trigger event parameters.

Read more · Source: github-advisories

entry.title: Joro: Unauthenticated Cross-Origin Plugin Upload Leads to RCE (CVE-2026-53649)
entry.content:
vulnerability · rce · CVE-2026-53649

Joro proxy mode (versions ≤1.1.0) on Linux/macOS contains a critical remote code execution vulnerability (CVE-2026-53649, CVSS 9.6) where an unauthenticated, CORS-enabled local API endpoint at 127.0.0.1:9090 allows arbitrary plugin uploads without authentication. An attacker can exploit this via cross-origin requests to upload malicious plugins and achieve code execution. There is no indication of active wild exploitation, but immediate patching to version >1.1.0 is strongly recommended, or disable proxy mode if not required. As an interim mitigation, restrict network access to port 9090 to trusted sources only.

Read more · Source: github-advisories
